other_observers: none; I am the original finder. Peter and Ellen Derven oberved the male the following day.
description: Small striped finch with predominate brown and gray tones. Male crown was dark with blood red tones. Bill was sharply pointed, straight, and yellow with dark culmen and at base. Face at base of bill was blackish. Breast was washed with reddish pink and darkly striped on sides and flanks. One prominent whitish wing-bar and faint second upper wingbar.  Females were similar without pinkish-red wash on breast.Behaviors: Birds were feeding at feeder and then dropped to ground to husk seeds.Call: Not heard wellPlumage: adult
